title: quercus pubescens subsp. crispata
intro: Quercus pubescens subsp. crispata, also known as the Downy Oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus pubescens tree, characterized by its downy leaves and distinctive growth habit. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which are beneficial in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Quercus pubescens subsp. crispata have been reported to help in reducing fever, alleviating digestive problems, and supporting the immune system, while also exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
